title: Proposal
text: Alcohol-associated liver disease (ALD) disrupts normal immune and metabolic processes in the liver. In this project, we analyze single-cell transcriptomic data to investigate how pathway activity shifts across cell types, examining evidence of immune suppression and metabolic reprogramming in immune cells.

title: Single Cell Analysis of Alcohol Liver Disease
text: This project examines whether immune cell populations show distinct gene-expression changes in alcohol-related liver disease (ALD) compared with healthy liver and whether these changes may contribute to disease pathology. To answer this question, we analyzed human single-cell RNA sequencing data from 5 ALD liver samples and 5 healthy control liver samples. Using a Seurat-based workflow, we performed quality control, normalization, dimensionality reduction, clustering, marker-gene annotation, differential expression analysis, cell-type proportion analysis, and gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA). This approach allowed us to evaluate both changes in cell abundance and changes in cell-specific transcriptional activity across disease conditions. Our results show that the immune response in ALD is not uniform. Instead, different immune cell populations display distinct and biologically meaningful patterns of dysfunction. T cells showed the strongest transcriptional changes, increased substantially in proportion, and lost key cytotoxic effector programs. NK cells also showed major suppression of cytotoxic pathways and decreased in abundance, suggesting weakening of the innate cytotoxic immune response. B cells showed reduced humoral immune and complement-related activity. Monocytes and Kupffer cells showed evidence of inflammatory metabolic reprogramming. In contrast, conventional dendritic cells upregulated antigen presentation and T-cell priming pathways, suggesting continued immune stimulation despite broader immune dysfunction. Across the dataset, complement suppression was the most consistent shared signal, appearing across both immune and non-immune cell populations and suggesting widespread disruption of immune homeostasis in the liver. These findings support the idea that immune dysfunction in ALD is not simply a secondary effect of liver injury, but may actively contribute to disease progression. This interpretation is strengthened by hepatocyte findings, including reduced expression of key functional markers such as ALB and APOB. This may suggest impaired normal liver function in ALD. Final figures include UMAP visualizations, PCA plots, cell-type proportion comparisons, marker-gene dot plots, differential expression plots, and pathway enrichment figures, all of which help illustrate how immune-cell dysfunction and hepatocyte impairment interact in ALD. Limitations include the nature of the dataset and the need to account for partial immune-transcript contamination in hepatocyte analyses.

title: Single Cell Analysis of Alcohol Liver Disease
text: This project examines whether immune cell populations show distinct gene-expression changes in alcohol-related liver disease (ALD) compared with healthy liver and whether these changes may contribute to disease pathology. title: Single Cell Analysis of Alcohol Liver Disease
text:
2 Project roadmap (living)

Find dataset(s)
Identified and used GEO dataset (GSE236382) with single-cell RNA sequencing data from ALD liver samples, and added a matched healthy control dataset for comparison.
Define outcome + predictors / variables
Outcome: disease status (ALD vs Control)
Predictors: gene expression levels across individual cells, cluster identity (cell type), and pathway enrichment scores
Exploratory analysis + QC
Performed quality control filtering (mitochondrial content, gene counts), normalization, and identification of highly variable genes. Generated QC plots and ensured data quality prior to analysis.
Method 1 (required)
Applied Seurat workflow including PCA, UMAP, clustering, and marker gene analysis to identify liver cell populations and compare expression patterns between ALD and control.
Method 2 (optional/extension)
Performed differential expression analysis followed by gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) to identify significantly altered biological pathways.
Final figures + interpretation
Generated UMAP plots, marker gene dot plots, and pathway enrichment figures. Interpreted results to show increased immune activation and disruption of metabolic pathways in ALD.
Limitations + ethics + reproducibility notes
To be finalized; will include discussion of dataset size, global vs cell-type-specific analysis, and reproducibility through shared code and data sources.

title: Single Cell Analysis of Alcohol Liver Disease

Citations: references.bib

title: Results
text:
ALD is associated with widespread transcriptional changes across liver cell populations, with distinct gene expression patterns observed between disease and control samples.
Cell-type composition and behavior are altered in ALD, indicating that disease progression involves both shifts in cell abundance and functional state.
Immune cells show the most pronounced changes, with coordinated downregulation of immune-related pathways across multiple cell types.
B cells exhibit the strongest suppression of immune pathways, suggesting impaired humoral immune function in ALD.
Monocytes display evidence of functional reprogramming, characterized by decreased immune activity alongside increased metabolic (ATP-related) pathway enrichment.
Pathway-level changes are highly cell-type specific, highlighting the importance of single-cell resolution for understanding disease mechanisms.

Overall, these results support immune dysfunction and metabolic alteration as central features of ALD pathophysiology.

title: Results
text:
6 Interactive GSEA by Cell Type
To avoid stacking all cell-type-specific pathway enrichment plots vertically, this section provides an interactive viewer for comparing GSEA results across annotated liver cell populations. Use the dropdown menu or the previous/next buttons to browse enrichment patterns for each cell type.

Select GSEA plot:  Cell Type Proportions: ALD vs Control GSEA - B Cells GSEA - cDCs GSEA - Cholangiocytes GSEA - Endothelial Cells GSEA - Hepatocytes GSEA - Kupffer Cells GSEA - Monocytes GSEA - NK Cells GSEA - Stellate Cells GSEA - T Cells Immune Cell Transcriptional Disruption Immune Pathway Activation Heatmap 
Previous Next

Global gene set enrichment analysis comparing ALD and control samples across the integrated dataset.

title: Results
section: 7 Interactive FeaturePlot Viewer by Cell Type
text:
7 Interactive FeaturePlot Viewer by Cell Type
This viewer allows interactive comparison of marker-based feature plots used to validate cell-type annotations across the integrated UMAP. Use the dropdown menu or the previous/next buttons to browse marker panels for each annotated cell population.

Select feature plot:  B Cells Markers cDC Markers Cholangiocyte Markers Endothelial Cell Markers gdT / NKT Markers Hepatocyte Stress Markers Hepatocyte Markers Kupffer Cell Markers Monocyte Markers NK Cell Markers pDC Markers Stellate Cell Markers T Cell Markers 
Previous Next

Feature plots for B-cell markers showing where canonical B-cell genes are expressed across the integrated UMAP.
